evals.name: design-fintech-segments
evals.prompt: Help me design our customer segments. We're a B2B fintech (Series B, $20M ARR) serving finance teams in companies $10M-$500M revenue. We have a draft ICP of 'mid-market companies' but it feels too broad. Our objectives this year: grow ARR 50%, reduce CAC payback to under 14 months. What segments should we be considering?
expected_assertions: Should run scripts/segment_canvas.py
Should NOT accept 'mid-market companies' as a real segment (too broad)
Should propose 2-4 candidate segments with need-similarity, NOT just demographic split
Should recommend behavioral/psychographic criteria over demographic-only
Should recommend Kotler strategy appropriate for Series B (single-segment concentration or selective specialization, NOT full coverage)
Should suggest MAPDA validation as next step
Should NOT generate generic 'try psychographic segmentation' advice

evals.name: validate-vague-segment
evals.prompt: Quick check: my marketing team wants to define our target segment as 'forward-thinking SaaS leaders who value innovation and embrace new technology.' Is this a real segment? What's the verdict?
expected_assertions: Should run scripts/validate_segment.py
Should FAIL on Measurable (cannot size 'forward-thinking SaaS leaders')
Should FAIL on Accessible (no identifiable channel for 'innovation-embracing' people)
Should FAIL on Differentiable (every SaaS exec self-identifies as forward-thinking)
Verdict should be REPLACE
Should suggest revision: replace with behavioral + role-based segment (e.g., 'CTOs at $50M+ SaaS who are evaluating their data warehouse in next 12 months')
Should NOT just say 'this is a vanity segment' without explaining MAPDA failures

evals.name: validate-strong-segment
evals.prompt: Validate this segment: 'Heads of People at remote-first US companies (200-2000 employees) hiring across 3+ time zones, currently using Notion + Slack for onboarding without a formal HR platform.' Evidence: about 8000 companies match per LinkedIn data, reachable through HR community Slack groups + remote-work conferences (Remote Forward, RUNNING REMOTE), willing to pay $15K-$45K ACV based on competitor pricing, distinct needs vs traditional HRIS buyers (no payroll/compliance focus), our current product fits closely.
expected_assertions: Should run scripts/validate_segment.py with the evidence
Should PASS on Measurable (concrete count: 8000)
Should PASS on Accessible (named channels: Slack groups + conferences)
Should PASS on Profitable ($15K-45K ACV × 8000 companies × even 1% penetration = $1.2M-$3.6M Year-1 SOM, real)
Should PASS on Differentiable (distinct needs vs traditional HRIS)
Should PASS on Actionable (current product fits)
Verdict should be KEEP
Should NOT suggest unnecessary revisions to a segment that passes all 5 filters
